decision_point: In an adult with an acute, uncomplicated (non-displaced) sternoclavicular joint sprain, is sling immobilization or surgical reconstruction preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with an acute, uncomplicated (non-displaced) sternoclavicular joint sprain, is sling immobilization or surgical reconstruction preferred?
Options:
  1. Sling immobilization
  2. Surgical reconstruction
label: settled
label_rationale: Uncomplicated sternoclavicular sprains heal reliably with temporary sling immobilization and symptomatic care; surgical reconstruction is not indicated.
